item.title: Crystal structure prediction
item.description: Input Elements Number of atoms Output Stable structure (global minimum)

item.title: File format
item.description: CrySPY uses the configparser module to read input file, cryspy.in . cryspy.in consists of sections, led by a [section] header and followed by name = value or name : value entries. Section names and values are case sensitive, but names are not. Lines beginning with # or ; are ignored and may be used to provide comments. Accepted bool values are 1, yes, true, and on, which cause this method to return True, and 0, no, false, and off, which cause it to return False.

item.title: soiap in your local PC
item.description: soiap is Structure Optimization with InterAtomic Potential. It is suitable for testing CrySPY because of its fast structure optimization. See instructions to install soiap. In this tutorial, we try to use CrySPY in your local PC (Mac or Linux). The target system is Si 8 atoms. Assumption Here, we assume the following condition: CrySPY main script: ~/CrySPY_root/0.9.0/cryspy.py CrySPY job filename: job_cryspy soiap executable file: ~/local/soiap-0.3.0/src/soiap soiap input filename: soiap.

item.title: System requirements
item.description: You need (CrySPY + Python environment + structure optimizer) in your workstation, super computer, etc. Python Tested with Python 3.8.5 on Mac, and Python 3.8.2, 3.8.3 on Linux. Python 3.x.x COMBO pymatgen PyXtal (CrySPY 0.9.0 or later) Structure optimizer At least one optimizer is required. First-principles calculation VASP Quantum Espresso OpenMX (CrySPY 0.9.0 or later) Interatomic potential soiap LAMMPS Others find_wy (optional in CrySPY 0.

item.title: [basic] section
item.description: Name Value Default Description algo RS, EA, BO, LAQA Algorithm calc_code VASP, QE, soiap, LAMMPS, OMX Caluculation code for structure optimization

item.title: find_wy (optional)
item.description: CrySPY have utilized find_wy to generate a random structure for a given space group (symmetry). However, CrySPY employs PyXtal library for structure generation as default since version 0.9.0. You can skip to install find_wy in CrySPY 0.9.0 or later, but you may use find_wy. For CrySPY 0.8.x or earlier, find_wy is required to generate a random structure for a given space group. You can skip to install find_wy in CrySPY 0.

item.title: Searching algorithms
item.description: The following searching algorithms are available in CrySPY: Random Search (RS) Evolutionary Algorithm (EA) Bayesian Optimization (BO) Look Ahead based on Quadratic Approximation (LAQA) In a nutshell Random Search (RS) Random. Evolutionary Algorithm (EA) EA for crystal structure prediction has been developed by Oganov's group . We also employ EA in CrySPY, and support the following: Selection methods Tournament selection Roulette selection Elite selection Evolutionary operations Crossover Permutation Strain etc.

item.title: CrySPY
item.description: Installation of CrySPY is very simple. Just download it! Download You can put the source code of CrySPY in an arbitrary directory. Here, let us put the source code in ~/CrySPY_root/x.x.x (x.x.x means the version). Use git or the compressed file. Git $ mkdir ~/CrySPY_root $ cd ~/CrySPY_root $ git clone https://github.com/Tomoki-YAMASHITA/CrySPY.git x.x.x zip or tar.gz file Download the source as a zip or tar.gz file from GitHub release .

item.title: Interface
item.description: CrySPY is interfaced with several structure optimizers: First-principles calculation VASP Quantum Espresso OpenMX (CrySPY 0.9.0 or later) Interatomic potential soiap LAMMPS At least one optimizer is required.

item.title: Check cryspy.in
item.description: See Input file in detail. Let’s take a look at cryspy.in again. This may be slightly different depending on calc_code you chose. [basic] algo = RS calc_code = soiap tot_struc = 5 nstage = 1 njob = 2 jobcmd = zsh jobfile = job_cryspy [structure] natot = 8 atype = Si nat = 8 [soiap] soiap_infile = soiap.in soiap_outfile = soiap.out soiap_cif = initial.cif [option] [basic] section algo: Algorithm. Set RS for Random Search.

item.title: Script to run
item.description: Assumption Here, we assume the following condition: CrySPY main script: ~/CrySPY_root/0.9.0/cryspy.py Make script Let’s make a convenient shell script to avoid typing long commands over and over again. Here, we create the script, cryspy (any file name will do). $ emacs cryspy $ chmod 744 cryspy #!/bin/sh python3 -u ~/CrySPY_root/0.9.0/cryspy.py 1>> log 2>> err -u option (unbuffered option) can be omitted. You can put this script in your $PATH, or just use like bash .

item.title: Firsrt run
item.description: Make sure you have calc_in/ (cryspy) cryspy.in $ ls calc_in cryspy cryspy.in Then, run CyrSPY! $ bash ./cryspy or, if you move the cryspy script in your $PATH, just type $ cryspy At the first run, CrySPY goes into structure generation mode. CrySPY stops after 5 structure generation. If it worked properly, log would look like this. 2020/11/11 19:59:18 CrySPY 0.9.0 Start cryspy.py Read input file, cryspy.in Write input data in cryspy.

item.title: Submit job
item.description: Continue CrySPY continues the simulation if you have cryspy.stat file. Continue if you have crypy.stat Start from the beginning if you don’t have cryspy.stat Submit job Run CyrSPY again. $ bash ./cryspy Check log file. 2020/11/11 20:02:54 CrySPY 0.9.0 Restart cryspy.py # ---------- job status ID 0: submit job, Stage 1 ID 1: submit job, Stage 1 And also cryspy.stat file. ... (omit) ... [status] id_queueing = 2 3 4 id 0 = Stage 1 id 1 = Stage 1 CrySPY submitted two jobs for structure ID 0 and 1 as you set njob = 2 in cryspy.

item.title: Check results
item.description: Move to data directory. There should be a few more files. $ cd data $ ls cryspy_rslt cryspy_rslt_energy_asc init_POSCARS opt_POSCARS pkl_data cryspy_rslt: Result file. cryspy_rslt_energy_asc: Result file sorted by energy in ascending order. init_POSCARS: Initial struture file in POSCAR format. opt_POSCARS: Optimized structure file in POSCAR format. pkl_data: Directory to save pickled data. cryspy_rslt_energy_asc file is suitable for checking results. $ less cryspy_rslt_energy_asc Spg_num Spg_sym Spg_num_opt Spg_sym_opt E_eV_atom Magmom Opt 1 98 I4_122 12 C2/m -3.

item.title: Append structures
item.description: Of course only 5 structures are not enough to find stable structures. You can append structures whenever you want. Here let’s append more 5 structures. Edit cryspy.in and change the value of tot_struc into 10. $ emacs cryspy.in [basic] algo = RS calc_code = soiap tot_struc = 10 nstage = 1 njob = 2 jobcmd = zsh jobfile = job_cryspy [structure] natot = 8 atype = Si nat = 8 [soiap] soiap_infile = soiap.
